Binomial model - exercise

In this exercise, we fit a binomial regression model to the snow vole data.
In our data set, called ‘dati.mod.csv’, the column ‘CattSiNo’ has a 0 for traps were no captures were recorded, and a 1 for traps were at least one capture occurred. This is our dependent variable.
For the purpose of our exercise, we use the columns from 8 to 15 as explanatory variables.


dati.mod <- read.csv("data/dati.mod.csv")
head(dati.mod)
varexpl <- dati.mod[,8:15]
head(varexpl)
NA

Data exploration

First look at the dependent variable:

barplot(dati.mod$CattSiNo)

table(dati.mod$CattSiNo)

 0  1 
45 30

Now look at the explanatory variables:

summary(varexpl)
      Incl            Alt           copveg         NSpecie       media.rocce     mediana.rocce   
 Min.   : 0.00   Min.   :2711   Min.   : 0.00   Min.   : 0.00   Min.   : 33.33   Min.   : 28.00  
 1st Qu.: 8.00   1st Qu.:2718   1st Qu.: 7.50   1st Qu.:10.00   1st Qu.: 68.17   1st Qu.: 53.00  
 Median :12.00   Median :2725   Median :25.00   Median :14.00   Median : 81.67   Median : 67.00  
 Mean   :13.27   Mean   :2725   Mean   :25.72   Mean   :15.15   Mean   : 88.84   Mean   : 73.27  
 3rd Qu.:20.00   3rd Qu.:2728   3rd Qu.:42.50   3rd Qu.:21.00   3rd Qu.:102.17   3rd Qu.: 88.00  
 Max.   :38.00   Max.   :2744   Max.   :80.00   Max.   :32.00   Max.   :190.67   Max.   :212.00  
    sd.rocce           Esp.tr       
 Min.   :  5.508   Min.   :-126.00  
 1st Qu.: 23.359   1st Qu.: -42.50  
 Median : 41.065   Median : -17.00  
 Mean   : 54.620   Mean   : -19.49  
 3rd Qu.: 73.980   3rd Qu.:  11.50  
 Max.   :225.247   Max.   :  62.00

Explore the pairwise relationship between the Y and X variables:

with(dati.mod,
     boxplot(Incl ~ CattSiNo, main="Slope"))

with(dati.mod,
     boxplot(Alt ~ CattSiNo, main="Altitude"))

with(dati.mod,
     boxplot(copveg ~ CattSiNo, main="Veg cover"))

with(dati.mod,
     boxplot(NSpecie ~ CattSiNo, main="N species"))

with(dati.mod,
     boxplot(media.rocce ~ CattSiNo, main="Mean diam"))

with(dati.mod,
     boxplot(mediana.rocce ~ CattSiNo, main="Median diam"))

with(dati.mod,
     boxplot(sd.rocce ~ CattSiNo, main="SD diam"))

with(dati.mod,
     boxplot(Esp.tr ~ CattSiNo, main="Aspect"))

Cleveland dotplot


par(mfrow=c(1,2))
dotchart(dati.mod$sd.rocce,
         ylab = "Order of observations",
         xlab = "SD diam", main = "")
dotchart(dati.mod$sd.rocce,
         groups = dati.mod$CattSiNo,
         ylab = "CattSiNo",
         xlab = "SD diam", main = "")
par(mfrow=c(1,1))


# eliminazione dell'outlier
subset(dati.mod, dati.mod$sd.rocce > 200)
dati.mod.new <- subset(dati.mod, dati.mod$sd.rocce < 200)

# trasformazione della variabile x
boxplot(dati.mod$sd.rocce)

boxplot(log(dati.mod$sd.rocce))

dati.mod.new2 <- cbind(dati.mod, logSD = log(dati.mod$sd.rocce))
dati.mod.new2 <- data.frame(dati.mod, logSD = log(dati.mod$sd.rocce))
head(dati.mod.new2)
NA

Analyse correlations

library(ggcorrplot)
cor(varexpl)
                     Incl         Alt     copveg     NSpecie media.rocce mediana.rocce    sd.rocce
Incl           1.00000000  0.09536722 -0.3398769 -0.29709854  0.06858470    0.17330635 -0.02999734
Alt            0.09536722  1.00000000 -0.1707269 -0.28290285 -0.06472299   -0.17462382  0.09391137
copveg        -0.33987691 -0.17072686  1.0000000  0.82584864 -0.19255250   -0.18480363 -0.10001584
NSpecie       -0.29709854 -0.28290285  0.8258486  1.00000000 -0.13201860   -0.05794438 -0.11444815
media.rocce    0.06858470 -0.06472299 -0.1925525 -0.13201860  1.00000000    0.67934914  0.81417140
mediana.rocce  0.17330635 -0.17462382 -0.1848036 -0.05794438  0.67934914    1.00000000  0.22016883
sd.rocce      -0.02999734  0.09391137 -0.1000158 -0.11444815  0.81417140    0.22016883  1.00000000
Esp.tr         0.26761038  0.30640578 -0.2841598 -0.27680890  0.13230026    0.04729249  0.12317768
                   Esp.tr
Incl           0.26761038
Alt            0.30640578
copveg        -0.28415979
NSpecie       -0.27680890
media.rocce    0.13230026
mediana.rocce  0.04729249
sd.rocce       0.12317768
Esp.tr         1.00000000
ggcorrplot(cor(varexpl), method = "circle")

Fit the base model

We can now fit our ‘base model’, i.e. the most complex model including all explanatory variables that are not too much correlated.

fm1 <- glm(CattSiNo ~ Incl +  # BEYOND OPTIMAL MODEL
             Alt +
             copveg +
             # NSpecie +
             # media.rocce +
             mediana.rocce +
             sd.rocce +
             Esp.tr,
           family=binomial, data=dati.mod.new)
#, offset=Noccasioni)
summary(fm1)


Call:
glm(formula = CattSiNo ~ Incl + Alt + copveg + mediana.rocce + 
    sd.rocce + Esp.tr, family = binomial, data = dati.mod.new)

Coefficients:
                Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)   
(Intercept)   347.854941 119.135566   2.920  0.00350 **
Incl            0.064217   0.035059   1.832  0.06699 . 
Alt            -0.127579   0.043647  -2.923  0.00347 **
copveg         -0.019573   0.015226  -1.286  0.19860   
mediana.rocce  -0.017244   0.010142  -1.700  0.08909 . 
sd.rocce        0.006677   0.008037   0.831  0.40607   
Esp.tr          0.008297   0.008682   0.956  0.33927   
---
Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1

(Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)

    Null deviance: 99.099  on 73  degrees of freedom
Residual deviance: 84.009  on 67  degrees of freedom
AIC: 98.009

Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 4

Now, try to reduce the number of variables in the model - different models can be compared in terms of AIC:

RICORDA SEMPRE: i modelli si costruiscono su ipotesi direlazioni ECOLOGICHE, non guardando solo la significatività (AIC più basso è meglio) ad esempio si può partire dal beyond optimal (con tutte le x possibili eccetto quelle correlate) e da lì creare un certo num di modelli su ipotesi diverse

modello topografico: le catture sono influenzate soprattutto dalla topografia del terreno (Incl, Alt, Esp.tr) modello trofico: le catture sono influenzate solo dalla disponibilità di cibo (copveg) modello predatori: le catture sono infliuenzate dalla presenza di ripari (mediana.rocce, sd.rocce)

in ogni caso però, si mostra l’output del modello migliore MA si citano tutti i modelli utilizzati senza scartarli del tutto

# UP TO YOU!!!

fm1.noexp <- glm(CattSiNo ~
              Incl +
              Alt,
             # copveg +
             # NSpecie +
             # media.rocce +
             # mediana.rocce +
             # sd.rocce +
             # Esp.tr,
           family=binomial, data=dati.mod.new)
#, offset=Noccasioni)
summary(fm1.noexp)

Call:
glm(formula = CattSiNo ~ Incl + Alt, family = binomial, data = dati.mod.new)

Coefficients:
             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)  
(Intercept) 223.84374   96.18255   2.327   0.0200 *
Incl          0.06531    0.03077   2.123   0.0338 *
Alt          -0.08265    0.03534  -2.339   0.0193 *
---
Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1

(Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)

    Null deviance: 99.099  on 73  degrees of freedom
Residual deviance: 89.421  on 71  degrees of freedom
AIC: 95.421

Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 4

Interpreting the results

If coefficient (logit) is positive, the effect of this predictor is positive and vice versa.

```r
fm2 <- glm(CattSiNo ~ #Incl +
             Alt +
             # copveg +
             # NSpecie +
             media.rocce, #+
             # mediana.rocce +
             # sd.rocce +
             # Esp.tr,
           family=binomial, data=dati.mod.new)
#, offset=Noccasioni)
summary(fm2)

### Conversion to probability



<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->


<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtYmVnaW4gZXlKa1lYUmhJam9pWUdCZ2NseHVZR0JnY2x4dWJHOW5hWFF5Y0hKdllpQThMU0JtZFc1amRHbHZiaWhzYjJkcGRDbDdYRzRnSUc5a1pITWdQQzBnWlhod0tHeHZaMmwwS1Z4dUlDQndjbTlpSUR3dElHOWtaSE1nTHlBb01TQXJJRzlrWkhNcFhHNGdJSEpsZEhWeWJpaHdjbTlpS1Z4dWZWeHVZR0JnWEc1Z1lHQWlmUT09IC0tPlxuXG5gYGByXG5gYGByXG5sb2dpdDJwcm9iIDwtIGZ1bmN0aW9uKGxvZ2l0KXtcbiAgb2RkcyA8LSBleHAobG9naXQpXG4gIHByb2IgPC0gb2RkcyAvICgxICsgb2RkcylcbiAgcmV0dXJuKHByb2IpXG59XG5gYGBcbmBgYFxuXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtZW5kIC0tPlxuIn0= -->


<!-- rnb-source-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiYGBgclxuYGBgclxubG9naXQycHJvYiA8LSBmdW5jdGlvbihsb2dpdCl7XG4gIG9kZHMgPC0gZXhwKGxvZ2l0KVxuICBwcm9iIDwtIG9kZHMgLyAoMSArIG9kZHMpXG4gIHJldHVybihwcm9iKVxufVxuYGBgXG5gYGAifQ== -->

```r
```r
logit2prob <- function(logit){
  odds <- exp(logit)
  prob <- odds / (1 + odds)
  return(prob)
}

<!-- rnb-source-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-chunk-end -->


<!-- rnb-text-begin -->



<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->


<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtYmVnaW4gZXlKa1lYUmhJam9pWUdCZ2NseHVZR0JnY2x4dWJHOW5hWFF5Y0hKdllpaGpiMlZtS0dadE1pa3BYRzVnWUdCY2JtQmdZQ0o5IC0tPlxuXG5gYGByXG5gYGByXG5sb2dpdDJwcm9iKGNvZWYoZm0yKSlcbmBgYFxuYGBgXG5cbjwhLS0gcm5iLXNvdXJjZS1lbmQgLS0+XG4ifQ== -->


<!-- rnb-source-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiYGBgclxuYGBgclxubG9naXQycHJvYihjb2VmKGZtMikpXG5gYGBcbmBgYCJ9 -->

```r
```r
logit2prob(coef(fm2))

<!-- rnb-source-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiKEludGVyY2VwdCkgICAgICAgICBBbHQgbWVkaWEucm9jY2UgXG4gIDEuMDAwMDAwMCAgIDAuNDgxMDY0NiAgIDAuNDk4NTc5NSBcbiJ9 -->

(Intercept) Alt media.rocce 1.0000000 0.4810646 0.4985795




<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-chunk-end -->


<!-- rnb-text-begin -->


The relationship between logit and probability is not linear, but of s-curve type.   
The coefficients in logit form can be be treated as in normal regression in terms of computing the y-value.   
Transform the logit of your y-value to probability to get a sense of the probability of the modeled event.


<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->

```r
```r
predict.alt <- predict(fm2,
                       data.frame(Alt=dati.mod.new$Alt,
                                  media.rocce=mean(dati.mod.new$media.rocce)),
                       type = \response\)
tab <- data.frame(Alt=dati.mod.new$Alt,
           prob = predict.alt)
# tab
library(dplyr)

<!-- rnb-source-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

I seguenti oggetti sono mascherati da ‘package:stats’:

filter, lag

intersect, setdiff, setequal, union

```r
```r
arrange(tab, Alt)
plot(arrange(tab, Alt))
